behaviors.description: Quality of translation (grammatical coherence + meaning preservation) is model-output quality, not wrapper logic. Pairwise: Unit loses to Integration because a single-function test with fixed fixtures cannot expose semantic-quality failures; Integration loses to System/Agentic because stubbed wiring still passes while real model quality can fail; Counterbalance System vs Agentic: Agentic wins since correctness depends on real AI output, not just transport. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior describes one model decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.justification: A real NMT model call is required to detect semantic/fluency regressions; mocks or deterministic fixtures hide the failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run eval prompts against the real translation model and assert human/LLM-judge quality criteria (meaning preserved, grammatical output, not literal word-by-word artifacts) across a representative multilingual set.

behaviors.description: This is fallback orchestration on a 503 status. Pairwise: Unit loses to Integration because failure appears at module handoff (API client -> fallback translator -> response path); Integration beats System because exact failure is status handling/branching and can be reproduced with an in-process stub returning 503; Counterbalance: no dependence on model output quality. Rejection gate: System rejected since behavior text is about handling a 503 code, not real network timeout/socket behavior.
behaviors.justification: Need multiple modules wired (client, fallback, service response), but no real OS/network/model-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + fallback + SQLite dictionary with a stub primary client that returns 503; assert caller gets successful fallback translation and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: Behavior is correctness of dictionary lookup from real SQLite-backed fallback path. Pairwise: Unit loses to Integration because correctness depends on repository/DB interaction, not pure function logic; Integration beats System because local SQLite access is sufficient and no real external infra is required. Rejection gate: Agentic/System rejected because no AI output judgment and no OS/network/subprocess beyond local DB.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is in module+SQLite wiring (query/schema/data mapping), which mocks can hide.
behaviors.test_description: Use a test SQLite DB seeded with known phrase pairs; call fallback translator and verify exact stored translation is returned for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: This combines edit-distance computation and persistence of the metric. Pairwise: Unit loses to Integration because behavior includes storage side effect (compute -> metrics store handoff); Integration beats System since local in-process components and test DB can reproduce persistence failures. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because metric correctness is deterministic string math, not model judgment.
behaviors.justification: To catch real failures you must verify both algorithm output and write-path persistence together.
behaviors.test_description: Feed fixed primary/fallback strings, run quality tracker end-to-end with test storage, assert computed character edit distance and persisted metric record fields are correct.

behaviors.description: Behavior is orchestration when both translators succeed: choose primary response and asynchronously log comparison. Pairwise: Unit loses to Integration because failure appears across service orchestration + background logging boundary; Integration beats System because this can be validated with in-process async worker/test scheduler and stubs. Counterbalance System vs Agentic: Agentic rejected by behavior text because assertion is routing/logging correctness, not translation quality. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected since only one model decision point is relevant and no multi-agentic chain is required.
behaviors.justification: Need multi-module wiring and async side-effect verification; real model quality/network is not required for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translators stubbed as available, assert returned value is primary output immediately, then assert quality-comparison log/metric is emitted asynchronously.
